subject: There's a hidden cost to AI-assisted code. It's not what you think.
body:
Here's a technique you can use to move fast, but also responsibly. ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­

3M learners (https://learn.educative.io/e3t/Ctc/RG+113/cz5H404/VWfjky9krMgrW64L79f1lM4gHW11fj5c5KmHR7N1FQ0tC3prCCW8wLKSR6lZ3nsVvNYdF2gnb5jN7Dy5WJ7Pk-VN1LyxZq_jRN7W6Yd9q_5yS5WvW7J4TyV1BNWpdN53cN0cKnwPwW66tFh61KtB-WW2xpczW27NhrWVCNy606CvFyLW7G910B1dpMTnV8pqXN5BjqG_Vrf4wv4w_471VRh6Tv12plXVW43LCpq4Y7fSyW5lL1_h30T3ZRW4QlxW19gS7qtW82xCm-9ktc6RVQ0Ynh1Bqmh7W3dFjkG7lRJ3MW3FsK1k5v-s00N5FrSWT3B-LTW1wMzKc6SLlbbW61RJNV52RyYFW7rP9M_2Q5-RzN739-Swd-rsQN77wt9Wj5R1vW4-sBjd59ZBW_VxH8R95xBjgKf2MhY8C04 )

Денис,

A quick note before we get started: we're still celebrating our milestone of 3 million developers learning with Educative. That means you can get 50% off (or more) (https://learn.educative.io/e3t/Ctc/RG+113/cz5H404/VWfjky9krMgrW64L79f1lM4gHW11fj5c5KmHR7N1FQ0tC3prCCW8wLKSR6lZ3nsVvNYdF2gnb5jN7Dy5WJ7Pk-VN1LyxZq_jRN7W6Yd9q_5yS5WvW7J4TyV1BNWpdN53cN0cKnwPwW66tFh61KtB-WW2xpczW27NhrWVCNy606CvFyLW7G910B1dpMTnV8pqXN5BjqG_Vrf4wv4w_471VRh6Tv12plXVW43LCpq4Y7fSyW5lL1_h30T3ZRW4QlxW19gS7qtW82xCm-9ktc6RVQ0Ynh1Bqmh7W3dFjkG7lRJ3MW3FsK1k5v-s00N5FrSWT3B-LTW1wMzKc6SLlbbW61RJNV52RyYFW7rP9M_2Q5-RzN739-Swd-rsQN77wt9Wj5R1vW4-sBjd59ZBW_VxH8R95xBjgKf2MhY8C04 ) on all Educative subscriptions—which all include access Fenzo.

What's Fenzo, you ask? Well that's what I wanted to talk about today.

AI is a double edged sword.

I'm not talking about ethics or hallucinations right now.

I'm talking about using it for learning.

For the most part, AI-powered workflows have helped developers move faster than ever. That’s a good thing, and it’s something I actively encourage people to embrace.

But learning with AI is trickier.

An overreliance on AI has been shown to harm skill retention (among other things). Studies from both Anthropic (https://learn.educative.io/e3t/Ctc/RG+113/cz5H404/VWfjky9krMgrW64L79f1lM4gHW11fj5c5KmHR7N1FQ0tW3prCCW95jsWP6lZ3kPW18cPP94_Cfk-W8QtNK_14yF_8VxtQSz6PKpv9W8fMB7x8VBJYvW6qHLLd6FbQJ1W4qTjnq3zRvltW6ckWnB1Zcvn6V93ygk8zzc_WW51jbg28KkhvQVJ2BbQ5XRfHgW5TLN6v5l778yVY7H2H5dZcP5W8xq3dP82BcFzW6kwMZS1j7F9pW4dSTH467bzzzW4pJpq_3VQgY2VKwYY_5bjNRlW6Zfwtc86gnsmW1XzJLD6XXBpkW1XF6tK4pq28cW4PK8v-94TgNCW2tfThz1PClRHW57HZ__7NwDzNW8mB4Cx87w_M2W9j6-qX93dqhBW7tr8t54bqZ72W2GbbSr6mK4lLV8Kbcr3rpQ-MW8lSpgy8KPy1CW82c4nS5mK_dtdn_8Yx04 ) and Microsoft have confirmed this.

The problem?

Most AI tools aren't optimized for learning; they're optimized for speed.

That design choice matters more than it sounds.

I'll explain why it matters—and how we flipped the script with our AI learning assistant, Fenzo.

Where generic LLMs fall short for learning

Generic LLMs are optimized to predict the next best response.

That usually means:

- Text-first explanations
- Jumping quickly to code
- Minimal structure
- No guarantee the answer maps cleanly to a broader concept

For quick unblocks, that’s fine.

But just because a generated snippet works doesn’t mean you actually learned anything.

To learn something effectively, you need:

- Context to fill a knowledge gap
- Visuals and metal models to solidify concepts
- Hands-on practice

This is what we designed Fenzo around.

Fenzo for depth and retention

If you’ve ever solved a problem with AI and felt a quiet sense of unease about not fully grasping the solution, Fenzo is here to give you the full picture.

When you ask Fenzo a question, it doesn’t treat it as a prompt to answer. It's built as a teaching system from the ground up.

Behind the scenes, Fenzo:

- Identifies the skill gap behind your question
- Follows a structured instructional sequence
- Generates quizzes or live coding environments to reinforce learning
- Anchors everything in Educative’s verified curriculum to reduce hallucinations

Building Fenzo was a major engineering effort for our team, and one of the highlights of 2025 at Educative. But the part I care most about isn’t the design — it’s how this shows up in real work.

A quiz at the end of a Fenzo response

Fenzo in practice

Say you’re debugging a RAG pipeline that’s returning irrelevant results.

You ask a generic AI what might be wrong. It gives you a plausible checklist: adjust chunk size, tweak embeddings, rerank results. You try a fix. It sort of works.

But you’re left wondering why it helped (and what you’d do differently next time).

That’s the gap Fenzo is designed to close.

Ask Fenzo the same question, and it doesn’t jump straight to fixes.

Instead, it:

- Walks you through how retrieval, chunking, and embedding similarity actually interact
- Shows you a mental model of the pipeline
- Lets you experiment with the code to see how changes affect outcomes

By the end, you’re not just unblocked. You’ve learned something you’ll reuse.

Being a developer means learning on the job. Most AI tools help you move faster—Fenzo is built to help you get better while you do.

What you can ask Fenzo

You can use Fenzo to:

- Debug in the moment
- Generate microtutorials
- Write and run code to apply concepts

You can use it to navigate everything from computer science fundamentals to System Design to AI skills.

Here are a few examples of the kinds of questions developers ask Fenzo:

- “Explain how RAG works and why my retrieval quality is poor.”
- “Help me build a mental model for async execution in this code.”
- “Why does this caching strategy break under load?”

There are still cases where a generic AI tool is the right choice. ChatGPT shines when you already have context and need speed—brainstorming, prototyping, or quick lookups.

Fenzo is designed for the opposite moments: when understanding matters because you know you’ll encounter the problem again.

Developers need both. We built Fenzo for the cases where learning shouldn’t be deferred.

Why this matters now

AI will continue to reshape how software gets built. That part is inevitable.

What’s still in our control is how developers evolve alongside it.

If we optimize purely for speed and don’t retain what we’re learning, the cost will surface years later in brittle systems, shallow intuition, and codebases that are harder to reason about.

With AI becoming a permanent layer in the developer workflow, we need tools that don’t just accelerate output, but strengthen understanding.

Fenzo is our answer, and we're thrilled our users have reported positive experiences with it so far.

👉 You can try Fenzo (https://learn.educative.io/e3t/Ctc/RG+113/cz5H404/VWfjky9krMgrW64L79f1lM4gHW11fj5c5KmHR7N1FQ0tj3prCCW7Y8-PT6lZ3pfW2D8P5M491gkWW4j17jk8kqs_JW2pK97Y9lbFL5W43swdn7zFqQ0W3n-kK65BF-9XN1jW_z3X4cZpW2_0yyP4SXWFHW7l9f5D4l6rKqW9dnhWP3VVGCtW8Vj5yt5p8Ts5W3sjQH476Cn3qV5HRR995cgLxW4V5NX23RJcr6N42L0gGXs0JmW3xT-bQ2B3QM5W6-j6wx5cdXwKN1Sh6dT62W_NW8mBRhn6Cb7jJVm9t6B6GG_M5W1m52Gw2VzrNKVB4fzh61GDmgN1dvtQBHbQvkW5cby_h10NJlWW2wTF_n5BWRHLW3VDMrd3qst3RW2ChprF84fjkjf5Nx3qC04 ) today with any Educative subscription at 50% off (or more) during our 3 million user celebration sale.

As always, if you give it a try, I’d love to hear how it goes. And if you’re already using Fenzo, hit reply and tell me how it’s been working for you.

Until next time,

- Fahim
